title: Mastery Exam: CSS Syntax, Scope, Cascade, and Flow
summary: Mastery Exam: CSS Syntax, Scope, Cascade, and Flow retrieves prior skills in an original independent base-style release decision scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css rules syntax, css application methods, css type class id selectors, css cascade origins layers, css inheritance initial, css box model, css display flow in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent base-style release decision artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Community Needs Survey artifact and connect styles.css. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Build the survey design system here. */

steps.title: 01 · Sequence 1: survey stylesheet parser investigation · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Reconstruct the verification workflow. Scenario: independent base-style release decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: CSS Syntax, Scope, Cascade, and Flow makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in independent base-style release decision.

content.text: Order the independent base-style release decision work by dependency and verify that each phase creates evidence needed by the next.

hints: Mastery Exam: CSS Syntax, Scope, Cascade, and Flow checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: CSS Syntax, Scope, Cascade, and Flow checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: CSS Syntax, Scope, Cascade, and Flow checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: independent base-style release decision phase 1.1: identify the selector and intended property-value pair
independent base-style release decision phase 1.2: inspect parsed rules and the first ignored token
independent base-style release decision phase 1.3: repair delimiters property spelling or value grammar
independent base-style release decision phase 1.4: confirm the declaration and computed value in the target state

options.text: Independent base-style release decision case 2: inline styles are best for reusable design because they have high specificity.
Independent base-style release decision case 2: use a shared external stylesheet by default and document any embedded or inline exception from concrete delivery constraints.
Independent base-style release decision case 2: use inline declarations for all component states because selectors should never be reused.

options.text: Independent base-style release decision case 3: more specific selectors are always safer and easier to maintain.
Independent base-style release decision case 3: choose selectors from stable component ownership and DOM relationships, then test intended matches and non-matches after content changes.
Independent base-style release decision case 3: encode the full ancestor chain in every selector so markup can never change silently.

options.text: Independent base-style release decision case 4: specificity alone determines every winning declaration.
Independent base-style release decision case 4: trace origin, importance, layer, specificity, scope, and order in sequence, then repair the earliest unintended winner.
Independent base-style release decision case 4: move every rule to the end because layers and origins never affect precedence.

options.text: Independent base-style release decision case 5: every CSS property inherits from the parent by default.

Independent base-style release decision case 5: identify whether the property inherits, inspect computed parent and child values, and use inherit, initial, unset, revert, or currentColor intentionally.
Independent base-style release decision case 5: repeat every text declaration on every descendant to prevent inheritance.

options.text: Independent base-style release decision case 7: display none hides content visually while preserving it for assistive technology.
Independent base-style release decision case 7: choose block, inline, inline-block, flow-root, or none from layout participation and accessibility consequences, then inspect both trees.
Independent base-style release decision case 7: set every element to inline-block so normal document flow no longer matters.

options.text: Independent base-style release decision case 8: browsers stop processing an entire stylesheet after one invalid declaration.
Independent base-style release decision case 8: locate the parsing boundary, inspect which declarations survive, repair the smallest syntax error, and confirm computed output.
Independent base-style release decision case 8: rewrite the complete stylesheet whenever one property is crossed out in DevTools.
